title: Hieracium pseudoflexuosum
taxonomy: Hieracium pseudoflexuosum is classified as a species within the genus Hieracium, which is a group of flowering plants in the dandelion tribe of the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The species Hieracium pseudoflexuosum is a member of the subgenus Pilosella and section Pilosella. This classification places it within the larger family of composite flowers and its related species. The classification of Hieracium pseudoflexuosum is subject to ongoing research and may be refined as new data becomes available. Further study is necessary to confirm its classification and relationships within the plant kingdom.

intro: Hieracium pseudoflexuosum, also known as False Flexible Hawkweed, is a perennial plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, providing relief from digestive issues, wounds, and skin infections, as well as acting as an antioxidant to protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium pseudoflexuosum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Hieracium pseudoflexuosum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
